title: feat(guard): redundancy / "this already exists" advisory (v0.16.0)
context:
Coding agents work from a local context window, so they re-implement
helpers and re-add code that already lives elsewhere. Hunch sees the
whole symbol graph: a diff that adds a function/class already defined in
another file is now flagged (advisory — never blocks) in `hunch check`,
the CI guard, and `hunch_merge_verdict`.

Tuned for precision and hardened via a 21-agent adversarial review swarm:
stopword + length filters, scoped to the change's own top-level project
root (src/ never trips over t Touches invariant(s): con_902759b3dc (All JSON writes to .hunch/ must be atomic (temp-file + rename) so an interrupted write can never truncate the index); con_a87360128b (Semantic vectors are a derived layer in SQLite, never the source of truth; reconcile by content hash on reindex).
decision: Introduced src/cli, src/core, src/store, test/checkreport.test.ts, test/redundancy.test.ts: added CheckRedundant, sym; new dep(s): node:test, node:assert/strict; 1 new file(s).
